title: Sample-question tests weakened to tautological assertions
evidence: test_server.py:672 (test_q3) asserts only isinstance(r, list); :717 (test_q10) and :794 (test_q22) same; :697-698 (test_q7) assert matches>=0; :748 (test_q15) asserts total_matches>=0 — all pass regardless of returned answer
suggestion: Assert on concrete expected content (non-empty results, known player/team) so these behavioral checks can actually fail

title: search_matches biased to first dataset; later competitions rarely returned
evidence: server.py:164 builds up to `limit` rows per source then server.py:205 returns results[:limit]; brasileirao fills the slot first, so team/either queries under-represent copa_brasil/libertadores/historic. The guard test was weakened from 4 to 2 sources (test_server.py:266)
suggestion: Interleave/round-robin across sources or gather then sort before truncating so all competitions can surface

title: Incorrect hardcoded team-name normalization mapping
evidence: data_loader.py:60 maps 'sport club do nascimento' -> 'flamengo' (an unrelated club); duplicate dict key 'athletico-mg' at data_loader.py:165-166; typo keys 'americ-mg' (:46) and 'goiases' (:59)
suggestion: Remove the nascimento->flamengo mis-mapping and the duplicate/typo keys; normalization should not fabricate identities to satisfy a test

title: Data-quality tests self-skip on non-numeric goal values
evidence: test_server.py:540 (test_numeric_goals_converts) and :579 (test_goals_non_negative) call pytest.skip when a non-numeric goal is encountered, silently bypassing the data-quality assertion
suggestion: Assert the value normalizes to a valid int instead of skipping so bad data is caught

title: No dependency manifest
evidence: run_dir has no requirements.txt or pyproject.toml; server.py:13-14 import pandas and mcp with no pinned versions
suggestion: Add requirements.txt pinning mcp and pandas for reproducibility

title: Aggregations iterate all rows per call with no caching of normalized names
evidence: server.py:269, :509, :606, :663 use df.iterrows() over the full concatenated match datasets (~24k rows) and re-apply normalize_team_name per call
suggestion: Precompute normalized team columns at load time and use vectorized pandas ops to stay within the <5s aggregate-query budget
